name: Ciudad Romana de Ercávica — round trip from Villalba del Rey
name_original: Ciudad Romana de Ercávica — ida y vuelta desde Villalba del Rey
region_intro: Road cycling around Villalba Del Rey features a landscape characterized by rolling hills, expansive reservoirs, and historical sites. The region offers varied terrain, from routes skirting the shores of large reservoirs like Buendía and Entrepeñas to paths that include moderate climbs. Cyclists can expect a mix of open countryside and routes passing through areas with historical significance. Best road cycling routes around Villalba Del Rey The most popular road cycling route is Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop, a 65.5 miles (105.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the Buendía Reservoir and passes by the ancient Ercávica Roman City. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Embalse de Biendía — circular por el Monasterio de Santa María de Monsalud, a difficult 71.8 miles (115.6 km) path. This route circles the Buendía Reservoir and includes a visit to the Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud. Local road cyclists also love the Pastrana and Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop, a 46.5 miles (74.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Entrepeñas Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Villalba Del Rey is defined by reservoir views, historical landmarks, and challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, with more than 20 routes available. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villalba Del Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes around Villalba Del Rey?
A: The road cycling routes in Villalba Del Rey offer a mix of challenges. While there are 2 easy routes, the majority are moderate (11 routes) or difficult (8 routes), featuring rolling hills and significant climbs, especially around the reservoirs.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villalba Del Rey?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy road cycling routes available in the Villalba Del Rey area. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them ideal for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Which routes offer the best views of the reservoirs?
A: Many routes provide extensive views of the region's large reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014635660/buendia-reservoir-and-ercavica-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094496748/embalse-de-biendia-circular-por-el-monasterio-de-santa-maria-de-monsalud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Biendía — circular por el Monasterio de Santa María de Monsalud&lt;/a&gt; both offer stunning vistas of the Buendía Reservoir. For views of Entrepeñas, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014637928/pastrana-and-entrepenas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastrana and Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in this region?
A: Absolutely. Villalba Del Rey is excellent for long-distance road cycling. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094496748/embalse-de-biendia-circular-por-el-monasterio-de-santa-maria-de-monsalud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Biendía — circular por el Monasterio de Santa María de Monsalud&lt;/a&gt; cover over 115 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014635660/buendia-reservoir-and-ercavica-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt; is over 105 km, offering extensive rides through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Villalba Del Rey are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014635660/buendia-reservoir-and-ercavica-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014637928/pastrana-and-entrepenas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastrana and Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in historical significance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014635660/buendia-reservoir-and-ercavica-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by the ancient Ercávica Roman City. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094496748/embalse-de-biendia-circular-por-el-monasterio-de-santa-maria-de-monsalud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Biendía — circular por el Monasterio de Santa María de Monsalud&lt;/a&gt;, includes a visit to the Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting during a ride?
A: Yes, the area around Villalba Del Rey offers several scenic viewpoints. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Dam Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic views of the reservoirs and surrounding landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villalba Del Rey?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be hot, especially in the open countryside, but early mornings or late afternoons can still be enjoyable. Winters are generally mild but can be chilly.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Villalba Del Rey?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Villalba Del Rey. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, the stunning reservoir views, and the opportunity to combine challenging rides with visits to historical sites like Ercávica Roman City and the Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud.

Q: Are there any routes that include significant climbs or mountain passes?
A: Yes, for those seeking challenging climbs, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018716240/climb-to-the-ports-of-tabladillo-and-casasana-circular-via-the-monastery-of-santa-maria-de-monsalud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the ports of Tabladillo and Casasana&lt;/a&gt; offer notable elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110608279/entrepenas-reservoir-and-casasana-port-loop-from-buendia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir and Casasana Port Loop from Buendía&lt;/a&gt; also features significant ascents, providing a rewarding experience for advanced riders.

Q: What kind of natural features will I encounter on the rides?
A: The landscape is characterized by rolling hills and expansive reservoirs, such as Buendía and Entrepeñas. You'll experience a mix of open countryside, often with views of the water, and routes that may pass through more wooded or agricultural areas, offering a varied natural backdrop for your ride.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Villalba Del Rey area?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Villalba Del Rey, catering to various skill levels and preferences. This extensive network ensures plenty of options for exploring the region by bike.
tips: Beautiful town where it is said that its name comes from the name of dawn, due to the white facades of its houses.
The ruins of the Roman city of Ercávica from the year 179 B.C. approximately. Free visit until June 30, 2023 (normal entry 2 eur). There is no toilet.
